nativeRange.habitat: Coral-rich reef areas at depths of approximately 5–25 m; generally found singly or in pairs

diet.notes: In the wild, has been documented grazing primarily on filamentous algae, sponges, tunicates, and small invertebrates such as copepods found among the rocks; trophic level data suggests a herbivore-leaning omnivorous diet. In captivity tends to accept a varied diet including spirulina, marine algae, nori, high-quality angelfish preparations containing sponge material, mysis shrimp, and other meaty frozen items. Frequent small feedings throughout the day are generally advised. Constant grazing behavior is reported; aquarists are commonly advised to avoid scrubbing all algae off rockwork during maintenance so the fish retains its natural food source.

summary: The Xanthurus Cream Angelfish (Apolemichthys xanthurus) is a large Western Indian Ocean angel with a dark velvety body, bright yellow tail, and silvery-white dorsal outline. Generally hardy but aggressive; best in large fish-only or FOWLR systems.
careNotes: The Xanthurus Cream Angelfish is considered one of the hardier large angelfishes once acclimated, and is commonly described as adapting readily to aquarium life. Retail sources tend to rate care level as 'easy', but the species' requirement for a large mature aquarium, plentiful live rock for grazing, and its strong aggression toward tankmates push it out of beginner territory in practice — 'intermediate' is used here. Sources broadly agree on adult size: LiveAquaria lists 8 in (20 cm), Tropical Fish Keeping lists 6–8 in (15–20 cm), and FishBase records a max of 15 cm TL; the 15–20 cm range used here reflects the spread across captive and wild observations. Temperature shows a notable spread between aquarium and wild data: hobby sources recommend 22.2–25.6°C (72–78°F), while FishBase wild-habitat data records a mean of 28.2°C (range 25.4–29.3°C). The hobby-oriented range is used here as it reflects long-term captive husbandry experience, though the wild data suggests this species likely tolerates warmer water without harm. Tank size also shows source disagreement: most retail sources list 125 US gal (~473 L) as the minimum, but given the species' large adult size, aggressive territoriality, and need for extensive rockwork plus open swimming space, 500 L (with a footprint of roughly 150 cm or more) is used here as a cautious practical minimum. Temperament is rated 'aggressive' to 'highly aggressive' across sources — only one specimen per tank is generally advised, and conspecifics or other angelfish should be avoided. Reef safety: LiveAquaria rates 'with caution' and several other sources rate 'not reef safe' due to the species' tendency to nip stony and soft corals as well as clam mantles; 'with caution' is used here for cataloging consistency, but practical advice is to keep this species in fish-only or FOWLR setups rather than mixed reef tanks. Adding the angel last to an established system, after other tankmates have already claimed territory, is commonly recommended to reduce aggression. The species has been reported to spawn in captivity occasionally but is not commercially bred at scale; tank-bred specimens are increasingly available from specialist suppliers.
breedingNotes: Apolemichthys xanthurus is reported to be a protogynous sequential hermaphrodite like most angelfishes, with no external sexual dimorphism except a typical size difference between dominant males and females. Broadcast spawning behavior has been observed. Captive breeding has been achieved on a limited scale, with tank-bred juveniles starting to appear in the trade through specialist suppliers, but consistent home-aquarium reproduction remains uncommon.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed scientific name (Apolemichthys xanthurus, Bennett 1833), family (Pomacanthidae), order (Acanthuriformes), max adult size (15.0 cm TL), native range (Western Indian Ocean from Mauritius to India and Sri Lanka), depth range (5–25 m), and wild temperature (mean 28.2°C, range 25.4–29.3°C). Wild temperature noted but aquarium-hobby ranges used for waterParameters.

additional.name: Tropical Fish Keeping
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (6–8 in / 15–20 cm), lifespan (up to 10 years), tank minimum (125 gal), temperature (72–78°F), pH, salinity, dKH, diet (primarily herbivorous in wild), and aggressive temperament. Notes constant-grazing behavior and broadcast spawning.

fish.breedingNotes: Protogynous hermaphrodite; broadcast spawner with no external sexual dimorphism. Captive breeding is uncommon; tank-bred specimens have begun appearing through specialist suppliers but home-aquarium reproduction is rare.
